references.title: Does the group matter? Effects of trust, cultural diversity, and group formation on engagement in group work in higher education

extracted.Courses: 6 Universities in Canada and the Netherlands
extracted.Features: [Trust in Group, Group Formation, Behavioral Engagement, Cognitive Engagement, Cultural Diversity, Country of University]
extracted.Preprocessing Details: The features (referred to as measures) are extracted from the questionnaire using a 7-point likert scale. Authors also performed exploratory and confirmatory factor analysis on the dataset (each on half of the dataset).
extracted.Groups Created: Authors find that the best model is the onre that includes a binary flag for country of university even though the parameter itself was not found to be signifcant.
extracted.Cluster Description:
Trust in group is biggest contributor to behavioral and cognitive engagement.

Cultural Diversity has a direct positive effect in both engagements.

Group formation has a small total effect, but a relevant direct and positive effect on cognitive engagement.
extracted.Implications:
Since trust in group is an important predictor of effort and,m in HE, should be fostered from the get go →> teambuilding activities may be helpful here.

Cultural diversity also promotes engagement even though they do not develop naturally… However, introduction of cultural diversity needs to be propoerly done, otherwise it will contribute to division rather than cooperation. The authors suggest some form of constrained choice could be effective at this.

Students should be given time to be acquainted to one another, especially in multi-cultural settings.
extracted.Comments:
This is a descriptive study for the context of Collaborative Learning. 

Authors created groups of at least 3 students and requested them to perform multiple different actions.

Authors explore effects of heterogenous groups and homogenous groups and used SEM and find that, indeed, these constructs have an effect on engagement in colaborative learning, which naturally also ties into performance and leearning from the collaborative experience.
extracted.Limitations:
Only captures a snapshot (cross-sectional), without delving into the mechanisms by which, for example, trust develops (a more longitudinal approach).

Use of Self-Reports.

annotations.text: Study wants to study factors that promote group engagement and teamwork by measuring how cultural diversity, trust and group formation affect team performance. Data is samples from a Questionnaire and uses SEM to identify the most likely predictors of of behavioral and cognitive engagement.
